WARNING: This data is ONLY for CSE and CS branch.
course_objectives: To familiarize the graduate engineers with the concept of Statistics and Probability.
To analyze practical/real life problems and solve them in scientific manner.
course_outcomes: CO1: Employ the concept of measure central tendency and regression analysis.
CO2: Apply knowledge of probability on distribution function.
CO3: Apply the concept of probability density function and normal distribution.
CO4: Apply the concept of random variable and time series.
CO5: Employ the knowledge of hypothesis by means of Chi-square and ANOVA test.

units.title: Basic Statistics
topics: Fitting of Straight line and exponential curve
Correlation, Rank correlation and Regression Analysis

units.title: Bivariate Random Variable and Time Series
topics: Introduction to two dimensional random variable
Joint probability density function and its properties
Measure of trend: Graphic method, method of Averages

units.title: Sampling Theory
topics: Testing a Hypothesis, Level of significance

textbooks: B. V. Ramana - Higher Engineering Mathematics, McGraw-Hill, 2017
B. S. Grewal - Higher Engineering Mathematics, Khanna Publisher, 2017
R K. Jain & S R K. Iyenger - Advance Engineering Mathematics, Narosa Publishing House 2002
S. C. Gupta & V. K. Kapoor - Fundamental of Mathematical Statistics, Sultan Chand & Sons
reference_books: Seymour Lipschutz, John Schiller - Introduction to Probability and Statistics, McGraw Hill
Peter V. O'Neil - Advance Engineering Mathematics, Thomson (Cengage) Learning, 2007
TKV Iyenger, B. Krishna Gandhi - Probability and Statistics, S. Chand Publishing House
E. Kreyszig - Advance Engineering Mathematics, John Wiley & Sons, 2005
